dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (ya as intention-confirm tag)
notes: Sentence-final 'ya' announcing a personal action and inviting acknowledgment — 'I'll do X, ok with you?'. The English 'yeah?' captures the soft-check register. NMT will likely render 'aku tunggu' as 'I'll wait' (future-ish from context); idempotency depends on that. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'alright?' might be too declarative; 'mm?' or 'OK?' could be more natural depending on dialect.
